approved_actions.description: Old setup doc in project root — completed 55+ days ago, 1 commit only
Reference doc in docs/ root, not in subdirectory. Should be in docs/reference/. NOTE: CLAUDE.md:35 links to this file — update link atomically with move.
Incident report in docs/ root, not in subdirectory. Should be in docs/reference/ (active safety reference).
Incident report in docs/ root, not in subdirectory. Should be in docs/reference/ (CLAUDE.md:18 directly references April incident).
SENSITIVE DOCUMENT: server-infra.md in docs/ root contains credentials/passwords (lines 17-22). Also misplaced — should be in docs/reference/. Security review required before move.
Orphaned commit-manifest JSON in docs/ root — should be in docs/dev/ where other cycle manifests live
Old completion doc in docs/dev/ — from 2026-01-07, >130 days old. Part of 7 completion-dev-* files from January 2026.
Legacy BA spec in docs/dev/ — 31 days old. Archive candidate if task is complete.
Legacy BA spec in docs/dev/ — 31 days old. Archive candidate if task is complete.
Overnight session state file at project root — tracked in git, session completed on 2026-05-03 (14 days ago)
7 completion-dev-* .md files from January 2026 in docs/dev/ — 130+ days old, no active task dependency
Workflow session artifact tracked in git — 53 days old (2026-03-25). Runtime state should not be version-controlled.
Workflow session artifact tracked in git — 31 days old (2026-04-16).
Workflow session artifact tracked in git — 52 days old (2026-03-26).
Workflow session artifact tracked in git — 28 days old (2026-04-19).
Stats cache file tracked in git — 54 days old (2026-03-24). Runtime cache should not be version-controlled.
Playwright config tracked in git — 53 days old (2026-03-25). Review whether this is intentional functional config or stale runtime artifact.
Hardcoded project-relative output path prefix 'docs/dev/overnight/' embedded in spec_path field of output JSON. The sid variable is parameterized but the base output directory is not.
spec_path base output directory is hardcoded as 'docs/dev/overnight'; only sid is parameterized. The output root directory should be a CLI/env/config parameter.
Hardcoded absolute path '/root/.claude/hooks' inserted into sys.path. This breaks portability on any system where the hooks directory is not at /root/.claude/hooks.
Hardcoded fallback path '/root/docs/dev/overnight' used as a candidate base directory. Breaks portability on any system where the project root is not /root.
Hardcoded fallback path '/root' used when CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR and os.getcwd() both fail. Any environment where the project is not under /root will silently use an incorrect project_dir.

Docstring contains environment-specific absolute path: 'Mirrors /root/knowledge-system/scripts/todo/ask.py structure.' — the /root/ prefix is machine-specific and will be wrong on any non-root environment.
Direct python3 call without venv activation: exec python3 "$PY_SCRIPT" --session-id "$SESSION_ID"
Direct python3 call without venv activation: exec python3 "$PY_SCRIPT" "$@"
Hardcoded staleness thresholds: 7 days (FULL_UPDATE_REQUIRED) and 3 days (INCREMENTAL_UPDATE) with no CLI parameter or environment variable override.
FOLDERS array is hardcoded with no CLI parameter to override or extend the list for different project layouts.
find -maxdepth 2 is hardcoded in three places with no parameter override. Scan depth could reasonably differ between projects or folder types.
Frontmatter slice length is hardcoded as '1,5p' (first 5 lines) in the FRONTMATTER mode sed command with no parameter override.
Hardcoded /root as the final fallback path in resolve_project_dir(). This is an environment-specific path that silently fails on non-root Linux users and all macOS/Windows environments.
Directory path segments ".claude" (STATE_DIR) and "docs/dev/overnight" (CYCLE_DIR) are hardcoded with no CLI parameter or environment variable override.
The spec auto-detection directory "docs/dev/specs" is hardcoded with no parameter override.
Bash syntax error: 'for instruction in "$TESTS_DIR/instructions"/*.md 2>/dev/null; do' is invalid bash — I/O redirection is not allowed in the glob expansion of a for-loop header.
Hardcoded paths ~/.claude/commands/*.md and ~/.claude/agents/*.md used for command/agent reference checking with no parameter override.
The ~/.claude/commands and ~/.claude/agents paths are not parameterized and cannot be overridden via CLI argument or environment variable.
Final fallback branch name "master" is hardcoded with no CLI argument or environment variable override.
All three orphan-detection scripts duplicate get_last_modified() and share structurally identical local/global scanning loops, JSON assembly patterns, and orphan-detection logic.
Severity-classification thresholds are hardcoded in get_severity() functions in orphan-detection scripts.
Comment references decimal step number 'Step 3.5' in: '# Step 3.5 MUST complete before clean-inspect (Step 4)'
Hardcoded relative directory path 'docs/clean/rule-context-${REQUEST_ID}.json' — the 'docs/clean' subdirectory is not derived from PROJECT_ROOT.
Hardcoded numeric constant MAX_ITERATIONS=5 with no parameter override mechanism.
Hardcoded folder list KEY_FOLDERS=("agents" "scripts" "docs" "hooks" "commands") — the set of key folders is a project-specific configuration value.
Hardcoded directory path 'docs/dev/overnight/' — script only accepts optional [session_id] argument and has no mechanism to specify a different project root or state directory.
Hardcoded environment-specific absolute fallback path '/root' in resolve_project_dir() — this path is host-specific.
Direct python3 call without venv activation: 'python3 - "$QA_PATH" "$MODE" <<'PYEOF''. Standard 3 requires all Python invocations to use 'source venv' or 'source .venv' with no stdlib-only exception.
Bash code block with actual find/ls commands using concrete project-specific paths. Standard 1 prohibits inline bash/python code with 'find' commands using actual paths.
Inline bash script block with full shebang, set -euo pipefail, variable capture, deactivate, and exit logic. This is a complete executable script embedded in an agent .md file.
Inline Python block with subprocess.run calls, timeout=30, JSON parsing logic, and exception handling. This is actual implementation code embedded in an agent .md file.

Inline Python file-reading and validation-loop logic: reads context files with open(), iterates criteria with for loops, calls check_criterion() and validate_criterion() — actual implementation code embedded in agent .md.
Inline Python error-handling block for instruction-based validator execution: reads instruction, gathers context files, iterates validation criteria with for loop — actual implementation logic.
Full aggregate_results() Python function with real loop logic: sum() generators, priority breakdowns, set comprehensions for edge case coverage — complete implementation code embedded in agent .md.
Full analyze_failed_tests() Python function with real loop logic: iterates failed tests, extracts violations, generates recommendations — complete implementation code embedded in agent .md.
Inline Python/bash execution-mode snippets for Standard Mode and Fail-Fast Mode with actual for-loop logic and break conditions. These are implementation code blocks.
Full check_imports() Python function with ast.parse(), ast.walk(), subprocess.run() calls to activate venv and test each import — complete implementation code embedded in agent .md.
Three Python functions (has_argparse, has_json_output, has_exit_codes) each with open() file-reading logic — actual implementation code embedded in agent .md.
Bash cat/jq command with concrete path (docs/test/edge-case-analysis.json) followed by full validate_edge_case_coverage() Python function with set operations — actual implementation code.
Inline bash code block with actual execution logic in 'Step 1: Discover Files' algorithm section — not labeled as example.
Fenced bash blocks containing actual execution logic (spec-check.py invocations) in Checkpoint Marking Contract section — not labeled as example.
Fenced bash blocks containing actual execution logic (spec-check.py mark/waive invocations) in Checkpoint Marking Contract section. Same pattern as architect.md:394-409.
Fenced bash blocks containing actual execution logic (spec-check.py mark/waive invocations) in Checkpoint Marking Contract section. Same pattern as architect.md and product-owner.md.
Contains inline Python implementation code: a 47-line python3 heredoc stanza (manifest.json writing with fcntl locking, sha256 hashing, section detection) in Phase 1 Step 5.
Contains inline Python implementation code: a 40-line python3 -c stanza (verbatim self-verification with regex whitelist and line-by-line checking) in Phase 1 Step 6.
Contains inline Python implementation code: a 53-line python3 heredoc stanza (cp-state slot resolution with fcntl locking, agent_id matching, checkpoint writing) in Phase 2.

The entire changelog-analyst.md agent file consists primarily of inline bash and Python implementation code. The file is an implementation script embedded in agent markdown form rather than a high-level behavioral specification.
Inline bash code block with actual git commands (git log, git show, git log --reverse) that constitute implementation logic for Step 3 git root cause analysis.
Inline bash code block with find/grep/ls commands against actual project paths for affected files discovery — implementation logic not an example.
Inline bash code blocks invoking spec-check.py with actual --spec-id and --agent-id arguments — implementation logic for checkpoint marking.
Inline bash code block in Step 3 for script execution — contains actual bash invocation logic.
Inline bash code block with git diff commands in Step 4 verification — implementation logic.
Inline bash code block with python compile and npm build commands in Step 4 — implementation logic.
Inline bash code block for reference integrity check in Step 4 — implementation logic.
Inline bash code blocks for code quality checks in Step 5 — multiple bash commands with actual logic.
Inline bash code block for hardcode scanning in Step 5 — actual grep implementation logic.
Inline Python test template code block — full Python implementation logic embedded in agent file.
Inline python3 invocation with actual test execution logic — implementation logic not an example.
Inline bash permission check code block — implementation logic.
Inline bash for loop — multi-line shell logic in agent file.
Inline bash code blocks for checkpoint marking (spec-check.py invocations) — implementation logic.
Inline bash code block with python3 grep detection — implementation logic embedded in agent file body.
Inline bash code block for step numbering grep detection — implementation logic.
Inline bash find command for naming convention detection — implementation logic.
Inline bash grep for Chinese character detection — implementation logic.
Inline bash grep block for marketing language detection — multiple grep commands forming implementation logic.
The command embeds actual workflow shell logic in bash blocks across Steps 1-6, 9, 11, 13, and the Test Folder Initialization section.

Multiple bash code blocks contain executable multi-line scripts: venv+python3 call, mkdir + REQUEST_ID/TIMESTAMP variable assignments, scan-project.sh invocation with jq pipeline, large bash block with discover-folders and conditional for-loops.

Additional executable bash blocks throughout clean.md: orchestrator.sh call, plan-style-inspection.sh + jq pipeline, orchestrator.sh merge call, checkpoint.sh + git rev-parse, jq -s merge command, git status + git diff.
Bash code blocks contain executable logic: wc -l command to count spec lines, echo to .split-complete file, echo warning to .split-complete.
Bash code blocks contain executable logic: venv activation + python3 close.py; mkdir -p + echo audit log append with date expansion; TASK_ID variable binding.
Inline bash code block in command file executes audit log write: mkdir -p ~/.claude/logs and echo ... >> ~/.claude/logs/commit-overrides.log. This is actual executable logic.

Python code block executes actual grant creation logic: imports json, os, secrets, datetime; constructs grant dict; calls Path(grant_path).write_text(...). Inline Python implementation in a command file.
Python code block executes dispatch-snapshot manifest logic: imports json, os, datetime, subprocess; calls subprocess.run for two repos; writes manifest JSON file.
Troubleshooting section shows 'time python3 test/scripts/validate-xxx.py --project-root .' without venv activation.
Troubleshooting section shows 'python3 test/scripts/validate-xxx.py --project-root . 2>&1 | head -20' without venv activation.
Direct 'python3 -m py_compile tests/scripts/validate-venv-usage.py' without venv activation in the Syntax Validation section.

Direct python3 calls without venv activation in bash code blocks intended for agent execution: 'python3 /root/.claude/scripts/spec-check.py mark' and 'python3 /root/.claude/scripts/spec-check.py waive'.

Direct python3 calls without venv activation in bash code blocks: 'python3 /root/.claude/scripts/spec-check.py mark' and 'python3 /root/.claude/scripts/spec-check.py waive'. Same pattern as architect.md.

Direct python3 calls without venv activation in bash code blocks: 'python3 /root/.claude/scripts/spec-check.py mark' and 'python3 /root/.claude/scripts/spec-check.py waive'. Same pattern as architect.md and product-owner.md.

Direct 'python3 /root/.claude/scripts/spec-check.py check-in' call without venv activation in Phase 2 checkpoint generation.
Direct 'python3 - <<'PY'' heredoc call without venv activation in Phase 2 cp-state writing stanza.
The Tool usage section authorizes 'python3 -c stanzas' as permitted Bash usage, explicitly endorsing non-venv python3 invocations.
Direct 'python3 /root/.claude/scripts/spec-check.py status' call without venv activation in the Quick sanity test section.
Direct 'python3 /root/.claude/scripts/spec-check.py mark' call without venv activation in the Checkpoint Marking Contract section.
Direct 'python3 /root/.claude/scripts/spec-check.py waive' call without venv activation in the Checkpoint Marking Contract section.
Direct 'python3 -c' call without venv activation in Phase 1 dispatch manifest parsing stanza (DISPATCH_FILES=$(python3 -c ...)).
Direct 'python3 - <<'PYEOF'' heredoc call without venv activation in Phase 10 push-gate token writing stanza.
Direct 'python3 /root/.claude/scripts/spec-check.py mark' call without venv activation in the Checkpoint Marking Contract section.
Direct 'python3 /root/.claude/scripts/spec-check.py waive' call without venv activation in the Checkpoint Marking Contract section.
Direct python3 invocation without venv activation: python3 tests/scripts/validate-<feature-name>.py --project-root .
Decimal step numbering in TRIAGE and RETRO protocol sections: Steps 1.5, 1.7, 1.9, 4.5, 5.5, 5.7.
Non-integer step label: '### Step 4-bis (S4-bis): Specialist Auto-Degradation Check'. The '-bis' suffix is not an integer step number.
Decimal step numbering: '### Step 0.5: Execute E2E Flow (MANDATORY)' (line 168) and prose reference 'E2E flow errors from Step 0.5' (line 203).
Decimal step numbering: '### Step 0.5: Execute E2E Flow (MANDATORY)' (line 172). Same pattern as architect.md:168.
Uses decimal step numbering: '### Step 0.5: Execute E2E Flow on Both Viewports (MANDATORY)'. This is a real step heading, not an example or anti-pattern demonstration.
Uses decimal step numbering: "Step 0.5" — non-integer step identifier.
Uses decimal and lettered step numbering throughout: Steps 2a, 5a, 5a.2, 5b, 5c, 5c.0a, 5c.0, 5c.1, 5c.1.5, 5c.2, 5d — none of these are integers.
Decimal step numbering used: "Step 4.5: Write commit grant and dispatch-snapshot manifest". Steps must use integers only.
Top-level verdict branch identifiers use lettered sub-numbering: '1b.' (line 308, AC-deviation-PASS branch) and '5b.' (line 323, Codex parse failure branch).
Chinese characters: "整洁度判定范围 = 仅本次 diff 新增的 violation = NO 阻断 close. 预存历史脖污一律不管". Per Standard 6 Exemption clause: verbatim non-English user-binding quotes belong in docs/dev/ticket-*.md only.
Chinese characters: "整洁度判定范围 = 仅本次 diff 新增的 violation = NO 阻断 close. 预存历史脖污一律不管". Same pattern as cleanliness-inspector.md:60.
Chinese characters in agent execution rule: 'QA摇烂' embedded in English prose.
Chinese text in agent execution rule: "一切以用户需求为中心". Context: "The user's binding directive is verbatim: '一切以用户需求为中心'"
Chinese text in agent execution rule: "安全洞例外：即便在路径外也必修". Context: "Section 5.4 rule 2 verbatim '安全洞例外：即便在路径外也必修'"
Chinese character '探索' embedded in English sentence: 'Specialists' free-探索 is preserved'.
Contains Chinese characters in example text: '"UI设计师"' in a bulleted example of what roles a spec might mention.
Contains Chinese characters in an orchestrator view template comment: 'UI设计师统筹设计构思图形和动效的表述，BA设计代码实现，dev实现，QA验证'.
Contains Chinese character in a forbidden cp.action pattern example: '"verify BA didn't 降级 scope"'.
Contains Chinese text: 禁止 full revert — verbatim user quote embedded in agent file body (not in docs/dev/ticket-*.md where it belongs per exemption rule).
Contains Chinese trigger keywords: "又", "还是", "没修好", "第 N 次" listed as retry-detection strings in rule body.
Contains multiple Chinese phrases in rule text: "超出用户实际需求范围的修改提议", "超出用户实际需求范围", "扩张式措辞" embedded directly in rule body of out_of_scope_observations chapter.

Contains extensive Chinese text throughout the Tone/Mission section: section headings with Chinese (画像, 引导, 本职, 完美, 整洁), inline Chinese in explanatory text, full Chinese verbatim user directive at line 543.
Contains Chinese trigger keyword: "以前没问题" embedded in regression detection rule text.
Chinese characters "以及", "并且", "以前" used as detection keywords in rule text.
Chinese character "修复" embedded inline in Codex instruction text: "out-of-path 修复 dressed as in-scope".
Chinese text "用户需求实测满足" embedded inline in technical field description.
Chinese text "dev 偏离 BA spec AC 但用户需求实测满足 = PASS，但 dev report 必须显式记录 AC 偏离原因" embedded inline in rule text as spec reference.
Chinese text "右侧有空白" embedded inline as example in verification rule — an English translation is already present next to it.
Contains Chinese Unicode range literal [一-龟] in detection bash code block used as an actual grep pattern (not a labeled example).
Contains Chinese text "核心清理目标" embedded in an example JSON block under Standard 6 detection.
Contains Chinese characters: 谢谢, 好的, 明白了, 完美, 清楚了, 就这样, 好了, 写吧 — listed as natural-conclusion detection signals but not wrapped in USER_VERBATIM tags.
Contains Chinese characters: 应/不应 — appears in a rule prohibition (DO NOT use 应/不应 AC ladders) without USER_VERBATIM tag.
Contains Chinese text: 安全洞例外：即便在路径外也必修 — cited as 'Section 5.4 rule 2 verbatim' but not in docs/dev/ticket-*.md.
Contains Chinese text: 如果对用户体验和安全性以及整体代码仓库整洁程度不造成阻碍的，都不一定是NO的原因 and 用户需求 / 安全性 — cited as 'user's binding directive' without docs/dev/ticket-*.md reference.
Contains Chinese text: 将clean用的诸如style-inspector等加入close的steps中 — cited as 'Section 5.2 verbatim' without docs/dev/ticket-*.md reference.
Contains Chinese text: 整洁度判定范围 = 仅本次 diff 新增的 violation = NO 阻断 close. 预存历史脏污一律不管 — cited as 'Section 5.4 rule 3 verbatim' without docs/dev/ticket-*.md reference.
Contains Chinese text: 历史脏污 — used as a technical term without USER_VERBATIM tag.
Contains Chinese text: dev 偏离 BA spec AC 但用户需求实测满足 = PASS，但 dev report 必须显式记录 AC 偏离原因 — cited as 'Section 5.4 rule 4 verbatim' without docs/dev/ticket-*.md reference.
Contains Chinese text: 如果对用户体验和安全性以及整体代码仓库整洁程度不造成阻碍的，都不一定是NO的原因 — cited as 'Section 5.1 verbatim' without docs/dev/ticket-*.md reference.
Chinese text inline in BA dispatch prompt Goal field without USER_VERBATIM wrapping: "实现方式是最小最安全最完美最确定性地实现用户的需求，而不是扩大修复范围。一切以用户需求为中心。"
Chinese retry-signal phrases appear without USER_VERBATIM wrapping: "又", "还是", "没修好", "第 N 次".
Chinese text inline in BA dispatch prompt Goal field without USER_VERBATIM wrapping: "实现方式是最小最安全最完美最确定性地实现用户的需求，而不是扩大修复范围。一切以用户需求为中心。" (identical to dev.md:362).
Clause C regex pattern contains Chinese tokens without USER_VERBATIM wrapping: 自动|手动中断|不要问|不要打扰.
Chinese phrase "用户需求中心" (user-requirement-centric) embedded in prose paragraph without any wrapper.
Rule 8 prose repeats Chinese autonomy-directive tokens without wrapping: 手动中断, 自动, 不要问, 不要打扰 appear in an explanatory sentence.
Chinese parenthetical "自由探索" (free exploration) embedded inline: "specialists 自由探索 must NOT be reduced".
Chinese retry-signal phrases in list without USER_VERBATIM wrapping: "又", "还是", "没修好".

Extremely verbose agent file: contains 4+ full Python function implementations, a 50-line 'Example End-to-End Execution' narrative section, and multiple documentation bloat sections. The majority of 680 lines is implementation detail.
Verbose agent file: contains multiple Python function implementations, detailed example walk-through section, extensive Troubleshooting section, and 'Best Practices' section.

Three verbose mock-terminal scenario blocks spanning ~80 lines. The Features section and Safety Features section duplicate the same 4 bullet categories. The Notes section repeats content from Features. The script invocation is duplicated.

The command embeds large runtime context schemas (Steps 7, 10), expected JSON output schemas (Steps 8, 12), decision-tree pseudocode (Steps 9, 13), and a full subagent prompt template (Step 12, lines 393-421).
Steps 14-16 embed full verbatim sample output templates: ~70-line markdown failure report template, 5-option interactive menu block, and a 125-line completion report markdown template.
Trailing sections duplicate information already present in the step-by-step workflow and add non-actionable descriptive prose.
Phase 6 embeds a full 37-line markdown runtime report template. This is a runtime output format specification embedded as documentation bloat.
Phase 6 embeds a full 45-line markdown runtime report template. This is a runtime output prototype loaded on every invocation.

The command file is a verbose integration guide: the 'Playwright is PRIMARY' statement is repeated three times; three separate per-command usage examples; a Troubleshooting section with 3 issue/solution pairs.
Phase 6 embeds a 46-line markdown output template with placeholder bracket text for every field.

File contains 5 repetitive 'Common Prototype Patterns' sections, a 44-line JSX scaffold template, 4 repetitive 'Example Prototypes' sections, verbose emoji-heavy quality checklist, and marketing/non-functional closing line.

References paths under test/ folder but the test/ directory does not exist on disk. The actual test infrastructure is in tests/ (plural). These are broken path references that would cause runtime failures.
References stop-workflow-enforce.py as the script that releases workflow-enforce, but this file does not exist anywhere in the repository.
References pretool-wrapper-userintent.py as a PreToolUse hook that consumes the stop sentinel flag, but this file does not exist anywhere in the repository.
References ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/bin/spec-verify.py but the file does not exist in the main repo tree.
References scripts/write-e2e-enforce.sh which does not exist in the scripts/ directory.
References scripts/write-e2e-enforce.sh which does not exist in the scripts/ directory (same missing file as dev.md:141-144).
References scripts/write-e2e-enforce.sh which does not exist in the scripts/ directory (same missing file as dev.md and dev-command.md).
